name: Culhwch
description: Culhwch is a prominent hero in Welsh mythology, best known for his role in the tale "Culhwch and Olwen," found within the Mabinogion. His story begins with a curse placed upon his mother, Goleuddydd, by his stepmother, causing her to only be able to give birth after experiencing madness and wandering aimlessly. Culhwch is destined to seek out Olwen, the daughter of the giant Ysbaddaden Bencawr, and win her hand in marriage. To achieve this, he must first fulfill a series of nearly impossible tasks set by Ysbaddaden, who knows that when Olwen marries, he will die. Culhwch seeks the aid of King Arthur and his knights, embarking on a grand quest filled with perilous adventures and incredible feats of strength, cunning, and bravery. He gathers a formidable band of companions, each possessing unique skills and abilities, to assist him in overcoming the challenges set by the giant. These tasks include retrieving specific objects, slaying monstrous creatures, and performing seemingly unachievable acts. Culhwch's perseverance, coupled with the unwavering loyalty and prowess of Arthur's knights, ultimately leads to the completion of all the tasks. Ysbaddaden Bencawr is slain, and Culhwch wins Olwen's hand, fulfilling his destiny and securing his place as a legendary hero in Welsh lore. His story highlights themes of fate, the importance of companionship, and the triumph of good over evil through courage and determination. Culhwch represents the archetypal hero who overcomes insurmountable odds through bravery, resourcefulness, and the aid of loyal allies.
subtitle: The Hero Who Sought Olwen
achievements: Sought and gained the aid of King Arthur
Gathered a powerful band of companions
Completed all of Ysbaddaden's impossible tasks

keyMyths.title: The Curse and the Quest
keyMyths.description: Culhwch's birth is preceded by a curse, setting him on a path to seek Olwen. This quest requires him to complete impossible tasks set by her father, Ysbaddaden Bencawr. The tale emphasizes destiny and the hero's journey.

keyMyths.title: Arthur's Aid
keyMyths.description: Recognizing the difficulty of Culhwch's quest, King Arthur and his knights pledge their assistance. Their diverse skills and unwavering loyalty are crucial to the success of the mission. This illustrates the importance of camaraderie.

keyMyths.title: The Impossible Tasks
keyMyths.description: Ysbaddaden sets forth a series of seemingly impossible tasks, each designed to prevent Culhwch from marrying Olwen. These tasks showcase the hero's ingenuity and the combined strength of his companions. This represents overcoming adversity.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A translation of the collection of medieval Welsh tales, including "Culhwch and Olwen."
sources.text: A translation of the collection of medieval Welsh tales, including "Culhwch and Olwen."

sources.author: Sioned Davies
sources.description: A more recent translation of the Mabinogion, offering a fresh perspective on the tales.
